NOT a blocker but worth flagging: no HTTP 429 seen, because nothing I ran touched the network. The heavy mission deliverable (346-basket render vs /workspace/POLARIS/competitors on RACE) was NOT attempted — it is not runnable without a wheel assignment, and OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md action 5 records it as still open and still distorted by two root-owned run_s5_i3.py jobs (PIDs 958596, 966951) that the box driver cannot kill.

failures.symptom: MISSION-CRITICAL, CONFIRMED BY REAL EXECUTION (not a code read): a number computed by execute_python can never reach the decide LLM, so it can never reach the report. I built a REAL AnalysisNotebook with a REAL successful execute_python ToolResult carrying the value 1234567.89 in .markdown AND .insights AND .statistics, then called the only notebook accessor the outline agent feeds to the LLM. The value is absent from all of it: the LLM sees the single line `1. execute_python [OK] (1.0s) — <reasoning[:60]>` and nothing more. This means the W3 'un-bricking' of execute_python (commit 1b2e3c7) makes the tool RUN but leaves its output a dead end — the number still gets written from evidence prose instead of computed. This is the open P1 that keeps the tooluse wheel UNSIGNED, and it is real at tip efe5af3.
failures.evidence: src/polaris_graph/tools/analysis_notebook.py:142 summary_for_llm() emits only `f"  {step.step_number}. {step.tool_name} [{status}] ({step.elapsed_seconds:.1f}s) — {step.reasoning[:60]}"` — it never touches step.result.markdown/.insights/.statistics. It is the ONLY notebook read that reaches the prompt: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:1163 `self.workspace.notebook.summary_for_llm() if self.workspace.notebook else ""`. Probe output: `MAGIC in summary     : False`
failures.suspected_component: src/polaris_graph/tools/analysis_notebook.py:142 summary_for_llm (shared with react_agent) + the single consumer at src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:1163

failures.symptom: TEST-COVERAGE GAP: the 7 green tests prove the DISPATCH SEAM only — the real execute_python executor body is never executed by any test in the tree. All three dispatch tests call agent.registry.register(ToolDefinition(name="execute_python", ...)) with an inline async fake, which OVERWRITES the real executor under the same name. So 'execute_python works now' is NOT what went green; 'the dispatch hands a non-None client to whatever is registered under that name' is. The real executor at tool_registry.py (the code path that returns success=False, "No LLM client available" at :556, and the codegen/exec path at :601-629) has zero test coverage. The only other file mentioning the tool, tests/v3/test_react_agent.py:473, just asserts the string appears in a name list.

result.sign_off_reason: Three P1s remain, all CONFIRMED against real source at efe5af3 by independent read + reproduced test run: (1) execute_python computed values are a verified dead end — absent from the decide prompt, the cp4 checkpoint, and the entry-point return, so the tool runs but influences nothing while recording no gap (undisclosed unreachable result); (2) the 7 green tests prove only the dispatch seam — the real executor _wrap_execute_python (tool_registry.py:547-640) has zero test coverage anywhere, so the 'un-brick' claim is under-proven; (3) the orchestrator dispatched wheel/branch/worktree as the literal string 'undefined', blocking the mission deliverable and forcing read-only gating. No P0: the faithfulness gate and verified-render lane are intact — no unsupported number can render through these paths.

sota.gap: On the tool-use/compute axis this wheel is BEHIND ChatGPT deep research and Gemini: both execute code and feed computed results back into model context and final reports, whereas here a successful execute_python's value (verified present in .markdown, .insights AND .statistics) is discarded at every exit — summary_for_llm() strips it (analysis_notebook.py:142), the cp4 checkpoint strips it (outline_agent.py:519-527), and the entry-point return carries no notebook payload (outline_agent.py:2022-2049). The tool is currently strictly net-negative (burns a turn + OpenRouterClient + sandbox exec for zero observable effect). Additionally there is NO empirical SOTA evidence on the depth axis either: the 346-basket end-to-end render vs /workspace/POLARIS/competitors on RACE has never been run (blocked by the wheel-interpolation failure and the two root-owned run_s5_i3.py jobs, PIDs 958596/966951). Closing the gap requires (a) computed values reaching the decide LLM, (b) a [#calc:] verified-lane exit so computed numbers can legitimately render, and (c) the head-to-head benchmark actually executed.

root_causes.why: summary_for_llm() formats each step as '{n}. {tool_name} [{status}] ({elapsed}s) — {reasoning[:60]}' and never reads step.result.markdown/.insights/.statistics; it is the ONLY notebook accessor fed to the decide LLM (outline_agent.py:1163 -> prompt at :1182). Every other exit also strips the payload: the cp4 checkpoint dump keeps only tool_name/reasoning[:200]/success (outline_agent.py:519-527), the entry-point return puts gap_ledger/disclosures/ev-maps but no notebook entries into digest_stats (outline_agent.py:2022-2049), and to_entries() (analysis_notebook.py:163) is consumed only by the ReactAnalysisAgent path (graph_v3.py:502), never the outline agent. Net mechanism: W3's dispatch fix makes execute_python RUN with a real client, but a successful run records no gap (so nothing is disclosed) while its computed value is unreachable by both planner and report — an undisclosed unreachable result. Verified by real probe: value 1234567.89 present in all three result fields, absent from summary_for_llm() output.

All execute_python tests call _register(agent, 'execute_python', <inline async fake>) which overwrites the real executor under the same registry name, so the green suite proves ONLY that the dispatch hands a non-None code-model client to whatever is registered (mutation probe confirms those assertions bind). The real executor body _wrap_execute_python — the no-client guard returning 'No LLM client available' (:561), the timeout path, and the success mapping of summary/statistics/insights into ToolResult — is executed by zero tests: grep for generate_and_execute_analysis under tests/ returns nothing, and tests/v3/test_react_agent.py:473 is a name-list membership assertion only. The commit headline 'un-brick execute_python' is therefore proven only at the dispatch layer; the tool can still be bricked one layer down without any test going red.

root_causes.file: workflow orchestration script (external to repo) — wheel-name template interpolation
root_causes.why: The dispatch template substituted the literal string 'undefined' for wheel name, branch, and worktree path (verified: /home/polaris/polaris_project/undefined does not exist; no branch matches 'undefined'; the three real wheels are bot/outline-agent-box, bot/outline-tooluse-box, bot/compose-box). This made 'work only inside <wheel>' unobeyable, forced both tester and gate into read-only mode, and blocked the mission deliverable (346-basket render vs competitors), which OVERNIGHT_PROGRESS.md action 5 records as still open.

fix_plan.change: Make computed results visible to the decide LLM: add an outline-facing result digest to the notebook summary — for each SUCCESSFUL step append a capped payload line (statistics as key=val pairs, insights, then markdown truncated), cap env-tunable via e.g. PG_NOTEBOOK_SUMMARY_RESULT_CHARS (LAW VI, weight-and-consolidate not filter). Safest shape: a new method (or opt-in include_results=True flag) consumed at outline_agent.py:1163, so react_agent.py:7875's existing consumption of summary_for_llm() is not silently changed under it.
fix_plan.acceptance: Re-run the tester's real probe /tmp/probe_compute_reachability.py (REAL AnalysisNotebook, value 1234567.89 in .markdown/.insights/.statistics): output line flips to 'MAGIC in summary : True' and 'insights in summary : True'; the existing 7 W3 tests remain 7/7 green; a new test asserts a successful execute_python's statistics value appears verbatim in the string handed to the decide prompt at outline_agent.py:1182.

fix_plan.file: src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py:~1637 (step landing after tool_def.execute) + the tradeoff_modeler/[#calc:] verified-lane API
fix_plan.change: Give computed numbers a legitimate render path per the binding invariant: when an execute_python step succeeds with non-empty statistics, register those values as [#calc:] / ModelSpec candidates through the verified lane at the step-landing point, instead of relying on the decide LLM to launder them into outline focus text. Exploratory markdown stays barred from rendering; only the verified lane renders the number.
fix_plan.acceptance: An end-to-end run on a compute-requiring question renders the computed value stamped [#calc:] and passing strict_verify numeric; grep of the rendered report shows the number appears ONLY inside the [#calc:] verified lane, and the disclosure trail shows the compute gap resolved by the tool rather than 'written from evidence prose'.

fix_plan.change: Test the REAL executor body without overwriting the registry name: monkeypatch ONLY code_executor.generate_and_execute_analysis and call the real _wrap_execute_python. Three cases: (a) success dict maps summary/statistics/insights into ToolResult with _cite_inline evidence ids; (b) client=None returns success=False with error 'No LLM client available' (tool_registry.py:561); (c) asyncio.TimeoutError path returns the timeout ToolResult.
fix_plan.acceptance: pytest -q shows the new tests passing, and a mutation probe (e.g. break the statistics mapping at tool_registry.py:~633) makes at least one new test go RED — proving the real executor body, not a fake, is under test. grep -rn generate_and_execute_analysis tests/ is no longer empty.

fix_plan.file: workflow orchestration script (wheel dispatch template, external to repo)
fix_plan.change: Fail fast on missing/empty wheel variable: before dispatching tester/gate, assert the interpolated wheel name resolves to an existing worktree directory and git branch; abort the dispatch with a loud error instead of emitting the literal 'undefined'. Then re-dispatch this wheel's round with the real identity (evidence points to bot/outline-tooluse-box at /home/polaris/wt/tooluse).
fix_plan.acceptance: The next round's task text names a real wheel/branch/worktree; `ls <worktree>` and `git -C <worktree> branch --show-current` succeed and match; the tester is no longer forced read-only and can attempt the 346-basket render vs /workspace/POLARIS/competitors deliverable.
